incidents.vector: Network intrusion followed by mass exfiltration of internal files; perpetrators are the World Leaks group (rebrand of Hunters International) operating a pure data-theft extortion model.
incidents.impact: Roughly 8.5 TB of payroll, contract, financial and editorial communications were leaked on the group's dark-web site, including notes from a January 2025 editorial meeting suggesting outreach to Moscow over Zelensky-related coverage. Mediaworks has threatened legal action against outlets reporting on the data.

incidents.industry: Cross-sector (healthcare, financial services, professional services, technology)
incidents.vector: Multi-stage 'code of conduct' phishing emails sent through legitimate cloud email infrastructure delivered PDFs with attacker-controlled links; CAPTCHA-gated landing pages, fake compliance portals and an adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) flow harvested credentials and proxied authentication to capture session tokens, bypassing non-phishing-resistant MFA.
incidents.impact: Microsoft Defender Research observed waves between 14-16 April 2026 targeting 35,000+ recipients across 13,000 organizations in 26 countries (92% in the US). Successful attacks yielded valid Microsoft 365 session tokens, enabling immediate account access and follow-on business email compromise.

incidents.vector: Ransomware-as-a-service operation run by former Conti leadership from St. Petersburg; the defendant specialized in pressuring victims with stolen data, including weaponizing pediatric health records, and laundered proceeds via a network of Russia/EU/US shell companies.
incidents.impact: DOJ sentenced Zolotarjovs to 102 months in prison; from June 2021–August 2023 the organization stole data from 54+ companies, with documented losses of $56M (incl. $2.8M in ransoms) on just 13 of them and an estimated additional $13M in ransoms from 41 other victims. A government 911 system was knocked offline.

incidents.victim: Indian and Russian organizations (industrial, consulting, retail, transportation)
incidents.vector: Tax-themed phishing emails impersonating Indian tax authorities (Dec 2025) and Russian tax authorities (Jan 2026) delivered Rust-based loaders that fetched ValleyRAT and a previously undocumented Python backdoor, ABCDoor, which uses HTTPS Socket.IO C2 and runs under pythonw.exe to evade detection.
incidents.impact: Kaspersky logged 1,600+ malicious messages in early 2026 across multiple sectors. ABCDoor enables screen streaming, remote keyboard/mouse control, clipboard theft and limited file encryption — Silver Fox's first known expansion into Russian targets.

incidents.victim: VENOMOUS#HELPER campaign victims (80+ organizations, US/Western Europe/Latin America)
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — initial access broker / ransomware precursor
incidents.vector: Phishing emails impersonating the U.S. Social Security Administration directed users to compromised legitimate websites that delivered a malicious executable, which silently installed two legitimately-signed RMM tools — SimpleHelp for scripted/persistence operations and ScreenConnect for interactive control.
incidents.impact: Securonix tracked the campaign since April 2025; RMM dual-tool deployment ensures persistence even if defenders remove one. Telemetry shows attackers monitoring cursor activity to time hands-on intrusions, with apparent interest in higher-tier employees and cryptocurrency holdings.

incidents.vector: Intrusion into Instructure's Canvas environment via undisclosed initial access; the ShinyHunters group claimed responsibility on Sunday and stated it stole data spanning thousands of educational institutions.
incidents.impact: Instructure's CISO confirmed exposure of user names, email addresses, student ID numbers and inter-user messages at some institutions. ShinyHunters claims 3.6 TB of data covering 9,000+ schools. Containment required revoking privileged credentials and access tokens, disrupting customer-facing tooling. No financial data, passwords or government IDs reported stolen.

incidents.vector: Active exploitation of CVE-2026-41940, an authentication bypass in cPanel/WHM affecting all versions after 11.40 (released 2013), enabling unauthenticated remote code execution against control panel interfaces exposed to the internet.
incidents.impact: ASD's ACSC issued a critical alert confirming in-the-wild exploitation in Australia; patches were released 30 April 2026. Targeted sectors are not yet identified, but the vulnerability gives attackers full server takeover on a product widely used for shared hosting and small-business sites.

incidents.vector: Unspecified intrusion into Moldova's national healthcare insurance database; no ransom was demanded, and Moldova's Cybersecurity Agency stated it cannot rule out Russian involvement amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and Russian military posture in Transnistria.
incidents.impact: Up to 30% of CNAM's database was reportedly affected (stolen and/or restored from backup), exposing extensive personal and financial data on virtually every adult in Moldova plus financial details of partner medical providers. Payments and medical services were not disrupted.

incidents.victim: Delta Dental Insurance Company / Delta Dental of New York
incidents.vector: 2023 exploitation of a zero-day in Progress Software's MOVEit Transfer used by the insurers to exchange files with affiliates, business partners and providers; threat actors siphoned a large volume of files containing consumer non-public information.
incidents.impact: NYDFS imposed a $2.25M penalty for violating 23 NYCRR Part 500 and for failure to timely report. Stolen records included names, addresses, SSNs, driver's license numbers, financial account information and patient health information. All affected consumers were notified by March 2024.

incidents.vector: A Mirai-derived botnet of TP-Link Archer AX21 routers (vulnerable to CVE-2023-1389) plus open DNS resolvers performed DNS amplification/reflection attacks. Operations ran from infrastructure of Brazilian DDoS-mitigation firm Huge Networks; attack scripts used the CEO's private SSH keys, with each target hit for 10-60 seconds before rotation.
incidents.impact: Years-long DDoS campaign caused 'very, very large' attacks against small Brazilian network operators. Huge Networks attributes the activity to a January 2026 compromise of a developer environment and now claims a competitor is responsible; KrebsOnSecurity exposed the activity after a leaked archive surfaced.

incidents.vector: A group of AI hobbyists used credentials leaked by a contractor and guessed Mythos operating endpoints to gain unauthorized access to Anthropic's private cybersecurity agent on launch day.
incidents.impact: Anthropic stated the intruders did not run malicious prompts and appear to have only 'played around.' The incident underscores credential-hygiene gaps around tightly held offensive-AI tooling and arrives as governments (EU, UK AISI) actively assess Mythos for cyber-uplift risk.

incidents.vector: Unspecified payment-system manipulation that diverted funds during a foreign debt repayment scheduled to settle obligations to Australia.
incidents.impact: $2.5 million stolen from the Ministry; Sri Lanka has requested assistance from Australian law enforcement. Repayment workflow disruption affects sovereign-debt servicing.

incidents.victim: npm/CI/CD ecosystem (Bitwarden CLI users; Checkmarx supply chain)
incidents.vector: Malicious @bitwarden/cli@2026.4.0 npm package executed a multi-stage payload via preinstall hook, downloading the Bun runtime to run an obfuscated 10MB credential-harvester targeting AWS, Google Cloud, Azure, GitHub and npm tokens. The same operator (TeamPCP) also pushed backdoored Checkmarx KICS Docker images, GitHub Actions and VS Code extensions sharing C2 audit.checkmarx[.]cx.
incidents.impact: Self-replicating Shai-Hulud-style worm steals npm and GitHub Personal Access Tokens to backdoor every package the victim can publish, embedding persistence into CI/CD pipelines for long-term enterprise access. Checkmarx's KICS scanner was compromised twice in a month.

incidents.victim: Cisco Firepower / Secure Firewall (ASA/FTD) operators including FCEB agencies
incidents.vector: UAT-4356 (the ArcaneDoor APT) chained CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362 in Cisco ASA WebVPN to deploy FIRESTARTER, a backdoor that injects shellcode into the LINA process and persists by manipulating the CSP_MOUNT_LIST during graceful reboots, surviving firmware patching but cleared by hard power-off.
incidents.impact: CISA detected post-patching persistence on FCEB agency devices and updated Emergency Directive 25-03 to require new forensic collection and updates; joint advisory issued with NCSC-UK. Affected hardware spans Firepower 1000/2100/4100/9300 and Secure Firewall 1200/3100/4200 series, with implants giving full remote code execution on perimeter firewalls.

incidents.vector: Email-bombing pretext followed by Microsoft Teams chat from a fake IT-helpdesk account; victim was tricked into running an AutoHotkey-loaded patch that installed SNOWBELT (a malicious Edge browser extension) and chained SNOWGLAZE tunnel + PsExec, RDP, LSASS dumping via Task Manager and FTK Imager exfiltration of NTDS.dit/SAM.
incidents.impact: Threat actor achieved domain-controller compromise and exfiltrated credential stores via LimeWire. Demonstrates evolution of Teams-based social engineering with a custom modular malware suite hosted in attacker-controlled AWS S3 buckets.

incidents.vector: A leaked GitHub Personal Access Token published on a public-facing site enabled the Crimson Collective to access the victim's GitHub, run TruffleHog across repositories, harvest client secrets and authenticate to Azure cloud storage via Microsoft Graph API for data exfiltration. Attempts were made to inject malicious code into repos to harvest future secrets.
incidents.impact: Talos IR's first observed Crimson Collective intrusion. Attackers used legitimate developer tools and native cloud APIs to blend with normal activity; expired secrets and effective controls largely thwarted persistent backdoors. Quarterly Talos data also shows phishing returning as the top initial-access vector and attackers adopting AI-based site builders (Softr) to spin up credential harvesters in minutes.

incidents.vector: Russian operators compromised the Signal account of the German Bundestag President, consistent with a broader Russian campaign targeting Signal and WhatsApp accounts that German cybersecurity authorities flagged earlier in 2026.
incidents.impact: Attackers obtained access to a sensitive group chat that included Chancellor Friedrich Merz; the chancellor's own account was not compromised. Incident illustrates ongoing Russian targeting of secure-messenger account-linking workflows against senior German officials.

incidents.victim: Cuban Embassy in Washington (and 5,000+ Microsoft Exchange servers globally)
incidents.vector: Exploitation of Microsoft Exchange email servers as part of a mass campaign that compromised more than 5,000 Exchange instances in 2026; the Cuban Embassy intrusion enabled theft of diplomats' inboxes to gather intelligence on the U.S. naval blockade.
incidents.impact: Chinese operators obtained sensitive diplomatic correspondence relevant to U.S. military operations in the Persian Gulf, exposing how mass Exchange exploitation continues to enable targeted espionage.

incidents.vector: Personal information from the UK Biobank — a research repository of medical and genetic data from volunteer donors — appeared for sale on the Alibaba marketplace; method of initial breach has not been disclosed.
incidents.impact: Roughly half a million individuals' data offered for sale, raising concerns about the security of large-scale medical research databases and re-identification risk for participants who contributed sensitive health and genetic information.

incidents.victim: Twilio, LastPass, DoorDash, Mailchimp and downstream cryptocurrency investors
incidents.vector: Tens of thousands of SMS phishing messages in 2022 enabled the group to compromise IT helpdesks at major tech vendors; stolen data was used to perform unauthorized SIM swaps that intercepted MFA codes for cryptocurrency account takeovers.
incidents.impact: Tyler Buchanan ("Tylerb") pleaded guilty to wire-fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft, admitting the theft of at least $8M in virtual currency. He faces up to 22 years at sentencing in August 2026; he is the second confirmed Scattered Spider member to plead guilty.

incidents.vector: Attackers compromised Stryker's Microsoft 365 environment and abused Microsoft Intune to issue a wipe command across thousands of corporate endpoints and servers; Iran-aligned hacktivist group Handala claimed responsibility.
incidents.impact: Global manufacturing operations were halted across Stryker's footprint, with downstream impacts on hospital medical-device supply. The incident, referenced in CyberScoop's analysis of digital infrastructure as critical infrastructure, illustrates how identity-/management-plane compromise can produce strategic-grade physical disruption without any kinetic action.
